docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Advanced Armors

Adds standard armors with equippable protection stats, overlays, optional training requirements, and mod support. Features: damage resistance, movement/jump bonuses, fall protection, footstep sounds, armor mod installation/removal, and admin toggletraining command.

Advanced Armors - Fantasy Edition

Adds fantasy-themed armors with equippable protection stats, overlays, optional training requirements, and mod support. Features: damage resistance, movement/jump bonuses, fall protection, footstep sounds, armor mod installation/removal, and admin toggletraining command.

Advanced Banking

Comprehensive banking infrastructure featuring multi-tier account management (free, premium, VIP), physical ATM network with model customization, secure check writing and redemption system, item storage vaults with tiered capacity, automated interest accrual with configurable rates and limits, paycheck integration with direct deposit options, administrative oversight tools for account monitoring and management, faction-based transaction controls, and robust permission system for member access management across joint accounts

Advanced Bone Manipulation Animation

Advanced bone manipulation system that provides realistic roleplay animations through precise skeletal control. Utilizes ValveBiped bone structure to create natural-looking poses including surrender (hands-up), military salutes, crossed arms, attention stances, typing posture, and various gestures. Features automatic interruption on movement, jumping, weapon switching, or vehicle entry to maintain gameplay flow. Integrates with lia.playerinteract for seamless context menu access, supports timed animations with auto-deactivation, and includes comprehensive cleanup systems for map changes and character respawns. Server-authoritative with networked synchronization ensures all players see consistent animations across the multiplayer environment.

Advanced Car Spawner

Comprehensive vehicle spawning system that provides players with an interactive GUI menu to browse, purchase, and spawn various vehicles. Features include customizable car registration system, physical car crate entities, intelligent spawn positioning with collision detection, currency integration, and support for both LVS and custom vehicle classes. The system includes server-side vehicle management, client-side interface with scrollable car listings, and automatic vehicle ownership assignment through CPPI.

Advanced Corpse Looting

Comprehensive corpse management and looting system that creates realistic player corpses upon death with full inventory and money transfer. Features include: automatic corpse creation with player appearance preservation, dual inventory interface for seamless item transfer between player and corpse, bidirectional money deposit/withdrawal system with real-time synchronization, distance-based looting mechanics with anti-spam protection, multi-user corpse access with shared state management, automatic cleanup of corpse inventories on removal, and visual/audio feedback for all transactions. The system handles equipped items by unequipping them before transfer and supports both modern inventory instances and legacy item systems.

Advanced Crafting

Comprehensive crafting system featuring multiple crafting stations (forge, workbench), recipe-based crafting with time-based progress bars, attribute requirements and skill progression, tool dependencies, faction restrictions, knowledge system with recipe books, dynamic UI with progress tracking and cancellation, item consumption and output with randomized quantities, and automatic entity registration for crafting stations

Advanced Police Suite

Complete law enforcement management system featuring 23-tier police rank hierarchy with progressive salaries and equipment kits, comprehensive criminal code with detailed offenses and sentencing guidelines, advanced police computer database with real-time criminal records, warrant management, and serial number tracking, specialized police equipment including tasers, nightsticks, and finebooks, dynamic jail cell management system with map-specific configurations, police locker and NPC integration, dispatcher system for citizen help requests, internal affairs oversight, and extensive administrative privileges for police faction management.

Advanced Radio Communication

Comprehensive radio communication framework featuring handheld radios and stationary broadcast units with frequency tuning, encrypted faction channels, preset stations, range-based transmission, static noise effects, and Star Wars RP comlink compatibility. Supports both item-based and entity-based radio devices with real-time voice transmission, channel management, and secure communications for different factions.

Black Market Trading

A comprehensive underground trading system featuring an elusive NPC black market dealer who sells illegal weapons, contraband items, and restricted goods. The system includes dynamic inventory management with limited stock quantities, automatic restock timers that replenish supplies over time, configurable pricing structures, secret location mechanics requiring players to discover the hidden shop, waypoint/package delivery systems for illicit transactions, and administrative tools for managing the black market economy. Perfect for servers seeking enhanced criminal roleplay opportunities with an immersive underground economy.

CCTV Surveillance

Complete CCTV surveillance network system featuring physical camera entities (lia_cam) that can be deployed and linked to monitor mainframes (lia_cctv). Players can access live camera feeds through monitor interfaces with faction-restricted permissions, or remotely using Personal Access Devices (PAD). The system supports multiple mainframes, named camera networks, real-time feed streaming, and provides comprehensive surveillance capabilities for roleplay scenarios with proper access control and authentication mechanisms.

Cellphone Communication

Comprehensive cellphone communication system featuring physical phone items with unique phone numbers and area codes (408, 650, 925), voice calling with call management, SMS/text messaging capabilities, private phone chat channels, player interaction menu integration for direct calling, contact management through phone number discovery, and network synchronization for multiplayer communication. Includes realistic phone number assignment, call state management, and support for phone-to-phone communication with proper validation and error handling.

Character Progression

A comprehensive character progression system featuring experience points, level advancement, and a skill tree system. Players earn XP through various activities, level up to gain skill points, and invest in three distinct skill paths: Endurance (increases maximum health by 25), Combat Training (boosts weapon damage by 15%), and Engineering (enhances tool effectiveness by 15% and repair speed by 30%). The system includes skill dependencies requiring certain levels, full F1 menu integration for skill management, network synchronization for multiplayer functionality, and administrative tools for XP/skill adjustments. Engineering skill requires Endurance tier 1 as a prerequisite, creating meaningful progression choices.

Chess & Draughts Gaming

Comprehensive chess and draughts (checkers) gaming system featuring fully interactive board games with real-time multiplayer support, Elo rating system with K-factor customization, persistent game statistics tracking, wagering mechanics for competitive play, global leaderboards, draw/resign functionality, pawn promotion selection, and seamless integration with the Lilia framework

City Guide NPC

Implements a comprehensive City Guide NPC system that provides new player orientation and navigation assistance. Features include interactive dialog trees with multiple conversation paths, automatic waypoint marking for important city landmarks (Downtown, Central Park), detailed location information, and contextual guidance about the city

Class Ranking

Comprehensive hierarchical ranking system that manages player positions within classes. Provides promotion, demotion, hiring, and kicking functionality with tier-based permissions. Automatically assigns rank-specific weapons, clearance levels, and models. Includes administrative commands and privilege-based access control for class management.

Comprehensive Achievement

Full-featured achievement system with real-time progress tracking, visual progress bars, and administrative oversight. Supports multiple achievement types: kill-based tracking (zombies, players, NPCs with specific entity targeting), item collection milestones, playtime rewards, death tracking, and complex multi-objective challenges with nested progress tracking. Features persistent character-based storage via Lilia

Comprehensive Drug

Complete drug economy and gameplay system featuring cultivation (weed planting with growth phases), processing (drug processor machines), consumption with various effects (speed boost, damage reduction, stamina regeneration, melee speed, needs decay reduction, ragdoll recovery), overdose mechanics with configurable chances, addiction system, visual effects HUD, and administrative controls. Includes 6 drug types: Cocaine, Heroin, Weed, Meth, LSD, and MDMA, each with unique properties and processing requirements.

Comprehensive Injury

A comprehensive injury management system featuring multiple injury types including broken legs, bleeding wounds, head concussions, and pain effects. Each injury has unique side effects, healing requirements, and medical treatment options. Includes medical items, treatment minigames, and persistent injury tracking across player sessions.

Comprehensive Medal

A comprehensive medal award and display system featuring multiple themed medal packs (1942 RP, US Military branches, Police departments, Star Wars RP), persistent character-based medal storage with rarity tiers (Common, Uncommon, Rare, Exceptionally Rare), staff permissions for medal management, wearable medal slots (up to 5 medals), character profile integration, and admin controls for giving/taking medals with full network synchronization

Comprehensive Resource Gathering & Crafting

A complete resource gathering system featuring tree harvesting (spruce wood, sticks, tree sap, logs, wood planks) with axe-based chopping mechanics, ore mining from rocks (iron, gold, silver ore, coal, stone) for smelting into ingots, an interactive fishing system requiring poles and bait that yields various fish types (lake trout, bass, catfish, perch) plus junk items (old boots, trash), comprehensive crafting materials processing (raw resources into refined materials like iron ingots, wood planks, and weapons such as iron swords), and full stackable inventory management with configurable max quantities for all gathered items.

Computer

Comprehensive computer system featuring interactive computer entities with customizable desktop interfaces, built-in applications including Notes and Fakebook social media, web browser functionality, and game integration. Provides network communication for computer interactions, popup management, and extensible app panel system for creating custom computer applications within the Lilia RP framework.

Data Storage

Comprehensive data storage system featuring portable memory disks (64C-1024C capacity) with read/write functionality, password protection, and terminal/decoder integration. Includes physical disk entities, inventory items, and interactive terminals for data manipulation and storage.

Death Trooper Voice Scrambler

Advanced voice communication scrambling system for the Death Trooper faction. Provides secure encrypted voice channels that prevent unauthorized players from hearing DT communications. When enabled, non-Death Trooper listeners cannot hear the voice chat and instead hear randomized static/scrambling sound effects. Authorized Death Trooper members and uplinked players can communicate normally while maintaining operational security. Features real-time voice filtering, multiple scrambling sound effects, and visual voice indicators showing scrambler status.

Delivery Job

A comprehensive delivery job system that provides players with parcel delivery missions. Features include a dedicated Delivery faction, randomly generated delivery locations across different maps, time-sensitive deliveries with visual indicators, delivery van spawning with custom bodygroups, crate pickup and drop-off mechanics, distance-based interaction prompts, monetary rewards for completed deliveries, visual waypoint markers showing delivery destinations, cooldown system between deliveries, and proper cleanup of delivery entities on disconnect or faction change. The system encourages map exploration and provides an engaging roleplaying experience for players seeking delivery work.

Dynamic Event

Comprehensive dynamic event system that creates map-specific NPC encounters with automatic scheduling, configurable spawn positions with radius-based randomization, player count requirements, intelligent spawn validation using hull traces, staff commands for manual event control, and real-time player notifications. Supports multiple concurrent events per map with customizable NPCs, positions, and messages.

Extraction Flare

Extraction flare system that allows players to signal extraction points by throwing throwable flares. Includes configurable extraction positions per map, flare grenade items, visual effects, and network messaging for extraction coordination. Provides raid-style extraction mechanics with countdown timers and escape zone functionality.

Fence - Black Market Dealer

A comprehensive fence NPC system that provides underground black market services. Features include: Purchase stolen items and contraband from players at fair market values, Dynamic pricing based on item reward values and rarity, Full integration with the robbery system for all stolen goods, Secure transaction processing with inventory validation, Interactive dialogue system with contextual responses, Buy and sell interface with item filtering, Support for weapons, valuables, and illegal items, Anti-theft protection and transaction logging.

FOB Respawn

A comprehensive Forward Operating Base (FOB) respawn management system that allows administrators to place faction-specific respawn terminals. Players can respawn at designated FOB points based on their faction affiliation, with support for multiple spawn points per faction and map-specific configurations. The system includes client-server synchronization, administrative placement tools, and automatic respawn location selection.

Handcuffs & Restraint

Comprehensive player restraint system featuring handcuff items and weapons, rope-based tying mechanics, complete weapon and movement restrictions for restrained players, drag functionality with submodule support, blindfold and gag features, lockpicking mechanics, realistic restraint sounds, and robust hostage scenario tools for immersive roleplay interactions.

Hunger & Thirst

Comprehensive survival system that manages player hunger and thirst levels. Features dynamic food registration with customizable hunger/thirst values, stamina penalties for low hunger, automatic hunger degradation over time, and a variety of food items including canned goods, beverages, and MREs. Integrates with character factions and includes staff exemptions.

Interactive Looting

A comprehensive looting system that creates searchable container entities throughout the world. Features dynamic entity registration, skill-based access requirements (strength/luck attributes), multi-tiered rarity system (Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary/Unique) with weighted probability distribution, intelligent reward allocation with inventory management, customizable container types (cardboard boxes, crates, barrels, lockers, safes), cooldown timers for respawn prevention, audio feedback systems, and seamless integration with the Lilia framework

Job Recruiter NPC

Automated job recruitment system that dynamically creates recruiter NPCs for all configured factions. Each NPC provides contextual dialog options allowing players to join jobs (with proper faction transfer hooks and loadout updates) or resign (reverting to default faction). Features intelligent option visibility based on player

Limb Damage

Advanced limb damage system that simulates realistic leg injuries. When players are shot in the legs (left or right leg hitgroups), they experience a temporary sprint disability with configurable duration. Features include automatic healing after the damage duration, visual HUD indicators with red screen effects and text warnings, server-side damage validation with blast damage immunity, toggleable system enable/disable functionality, and proper cleanup on player disconnection. The system uses networked variables for synchronized client-server state and provides immersive gameplay feedback through notifications and visual effects.

LSCS Lightsaber

Integrates LSCS (Lightsaber Combat System) framework with Lilia, providing comprehensive lightsaber mechanics including customizable hilts, crystals, stances, and abilities. Features dual-wielding support, inventory management, and seamless character data synchronization for enhanced Jedi/Sith roleplay combat experience.

Medical & Wound

Comprehensive medical system featuring persistent wound tracking that reduces max health, a configurable medkit SWEP for healing self/others with cooldowns, and a surgery system requiring certified providers and surgical kits to treat lingering wounds. Players accumulate wounds on death that permanently decrease maximum health until treated through surgery.

Player Marketplace

A comprehensive player-to-player trading marketplace system featuring persistent database storage, configurable listing limits and pricing controls, intuitive GUI interfaces for buyers and sellers, administrative management tools with item removal and price modification capabilities, automatic economic transactions with money transfers, NPC vendor integration for marketplace access, real-time listing updates and search functionality, item validation and blacklist support, and robust server economy integration.

Prop-Based Building

Advanced prop-based construction system enabling players to craft and place diverse furniture items including chairs, tables, couches, storage units, and decorative props. Features resource requirements, health systems, destruction mechanics with specialized tools, and supports upgrades into functional entities for comprehensive base building and interior customization.

Realtor - Property Management

Comprehensive property creation and management system featuring a specialized Property Creator weapon for intuitive door selection. Supports temporary rentals with daily pricing and permanent faction-owned properties with database persistence, preview positioning, and multi-door property compilation.

Security Clearance

Implements a comprehensive security clearance level system that controls access to restricted areas through door permissions. Features include character-based clearance levels, administrative management tools, flag-based access control, and integration with the existing door system to enforce security restrictions based on player authorization levels.

Store Robbery

Comprehensive store robbery system featuring interactive jewelry cases and cash registers with timed robbery mechanics. Players can steal various valuable items including cash bundles (0-500 value), jewelry bags (100-300 value), gold watches (50-150 value), and diamond rings (200-500 value) with weighted probability drops. The system includes police requirements (minimum 1-2 officers), configurable respawn timers (15-30 minutes), cooldown states, inventory management, and faction blacklist support. Features visual feedback with entity information display, action progress bars, and success/failure notifications.

Taxi Transportation Service

A comprehensive taxi transportation system featuring an NPC dispatcher that spawns taxi vehicles for player hire, provides instant teleportation to predefined city locations with distance-based pricing, prevents duplicate taxi ownership, and integrates custom workshop vehicle models for realistic taxi services.

Territories - Faction Control Point

Comprehensive territory control system that allows factions to capture, defend, and hold strategic control points. Features include configurable capture zones with customizable radius and capture timers, automatic NPC defender spawning, real-time HUD status displays showing current ownership and capture progress, faction-based ownership mechanics with collision prevention to ensure proper point distribution, reward systems for holding territories, persistent entity saving, and administrative tools for managing control points. The system supports dynamic territory warfare where factions can contest control points through presence-based capture mechanics requiring minimum defenders to secure areas.

Terrorism

Comprehensive terrorism system featuring vehicle car bombs that detonate on engine start and door breach explosives. Both bomb types support timed detonation (0-60 seconds) and remote detonation using handheld detonators. Includes realistic explosion physics with debris creation, player interaction menus for remote detonation, and a complete item system with explosive devices and detonators. Perfect for sabotage operations, controlled demolitions, and asymmetric warfare scenarios.

Vehicle Dealership

A comprehensive vehicle management system that provides NPC-based vehicle purchasing, ownership tracking, and maintenance services. Features include interactive dealer NPCs for vehicle sales with configurable pricing, vehicle return and repair systems with fee-based services, custom paint jobs and bodygroup modifications with individual pricing, secure garage storage for owned vehicles, full integration with popular driving addons, administrative privileges for staff vehicle access, and persistent vehicle ownership data across server restarts.

Vehicle Entertainment & Passenger

Comprehensive vehicle enhancement system featuring CD player with music track management, album organization, and audio streaming for in-car entertainment. Includes passenger detection and management for both players and bots, supporting various vehicle types including LVS and SCars with proper seat identification and driver/passenger tracking.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.1
  • Added smooth blend-in/blend-out transitions for animations
  • Adjusted interruption logic (including crouch and lower movement threshold) and centralized animation updating
  • Added additional gesture animations (e.g., Peace Sign, Thumbs Up/Down, Pew Pew, etc.)
  • Added player interaction action to sequence/play all animations and ensured Stop Animation cancels active sequence
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Armor Generator Mod Generator Unique ID: Unique identifier for this armor (no spaces, lowercase) Armor Name: Description: Standard combat armor worn by Combine soldiers Icon Model: Shown in inventory (also defaults item model) Player Model: Applied to player when equipped (optional) Resistance: 0-100 damage resistance Speed Boost: Negative slows, positive speeds up Jump Boost:  Takes Fall Damage               Power Armor              Overlay: Leave empty for none Footstep Sound: Leave empty for nil Supported Mods: Each row is one mod type this armor supports (not uniqueIDs). Add Mod Generate Armor Code Generate Example Unique ID: Unique identifier for this mod item Mod Type: This must match armor "supportedMods" entries Mod Name: Description: Increases physical damage resistance. Effect Description: Model: Physical Resist: Magic Resist: Regen: Speed: Damage: Generate Mod Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Advanced Armors
docs.text: Adds standard armors with equippable protection stats, overlays, optional training requirements, and mod support. Features: damage resistance, movement/jump bonuses, fall protection, footstep sounds, armor mod installation/removal, and admin toggletraining command.

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Armor Generator Mod Generator Unique ID: Unique identifier for this armor (no spaces, lowercase) Armor Name: The name shown to players for this armor item. Description: Standard combat armor worn by Combine soldiers Shown in item tooltip/details. Icon Model: Shown in inventory (also defaults item model) Player Model: Applied to player when equipped (optional) Player Model Mode: Single Model Multi Model Multi Model uses an equivalency table compatible with lia.armors.resolvePlayerModel. Resistance: 0-100 damage resistance Speed Boost: Negative slows, positive speeds up Jump Boost: Adjusts jump height while equipped (module-defined scaling). Takes Fall Damage If enabled, normal fall damage applies while this armor is equipped. Requires Training If enabled, players must meet the module's training requirement before equipping/using this armor. Overlay: Overlay to apply while this armor is equipped. Leave empty for none. Footstep Sound: Footstep sound used while this armor is equipped. Leave empty for nil (use default footstep sounds). Supported Mods: Each row is one mod type this armor supports (not uniqueIDs). Add Mod Model Equivalencies: Each row maps a current model to a replacement. Use key default or * Add Model Mapping Generate Armor Code Generate Example Unique ID: Unique identifier for this mod item Mod Type: This must match armor "supportedMods" entries Mod Name: The name shown to players for this mod item. Description: Increases physical damage resistance. Shown in item tooltip/details. Effect Description: A player-facing summary of what the mod does (does not affect stats by itself). Model: World/inventory model for the mod item. Resist: Resistance value to apply via effects.resist when the mod is installed (module-defined scaling). Regen: Regeneration value to apply via effects.regen when the mod is installed (module-defined scaling). Speed: Speed modifier to apply via effects.speed when installed (negative slows, positive speeds up). Damage: Damage modifier to apply via effects.damage when installed (module-defined usage). Generate Mod Code Generate Example Generated Code

The system includes dynamic inventory management with limited stock quantities, automatic restock timers that replenish supplies over time, configurable pricing structures, secret location mechanics requiring players to discover the hidden shop, waypoint/package delivery systems for illicit transactions, and administrative tools for managing the black market economy. docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Unique ID: Unique identifier for this item (no spaces, lowercase) Display Name: Name shown in the blackmarket UI. Description: An unregistered firearm commonly sold on the blackmarket. Buyer assumes all responsibility. Longer description shown in the UI and/or tooltip. Base Price: Default/base price in dollars. Cooldown (seconds): Individual item cooldown between purchases/orders (module-defined behavior). Max Quantity: Maximum quantity available (0 = unlimited). Category: Used to group items in the blackmarket UI. Use Faction Restrictions If enabled, restrict purchasing by faction (whitelist) and/or override pricing per faction. Restriction Type: Whitelist (Only allowed factions) Faction-Specific Pricing Whitelist: only listed factions can buy. Faction-Specific Pricing: override base price per faction. Allowed Factions: Each row is one faction constant (e.g., FACTION_CITIZEN). Add Faction Faction Pricing: Each row overrides the base price for one faction constant (e.g., FACTION_CITIZEN). Add Faction Price Generate Blackmarket Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Advanced Bonemerge Clothing
docs.text: Comprehensive bonemerge-based clothing system that allows players to equip and customize outfits with multiple clothing slots. Features dynamic model merging, slot-based inventory management, gender-specific clothing, VIP items, plastic surgery, and a complete vendor system for purchasing and managing clothing items including hats, shirts, pants, shoes, accessories, and full outfits.

docs.title: Vehicle Dealership
docs.text: A comprehensive vehicle management system that provides NPC-based vehicle purchasing, ownership tracking, and maintenance services. Features include interactive dealer NPCs for vehicle sales with configurable pricing, vehicle return and repair systems with fee-based services, custom paint jobs and bodygroup modifications with individual pricing, secure garage storage for owned vehicles, full integration with popular driving addons, administrative privileges for staff vehicle access, and persistent vehicle ownership data across server restarts.

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Car Class: Vehicle entity class name (used as unique identifier) Vehicle Name: Name shown to players in the vehicle dealer UI. Category: Shown as the tab/category in the dealer UI Vehicle Model: 3D model path for the vehicle. For simfphys vehicles, this is usually auto-retrieved and can be left empty. Price: Purchase price in currency Simfphys Vehicle: Auto-detect Force Yes Force No Auto-detects for classes starting with simfphys_ or sim_fphys_ Special / No Buy: No Yes Yes/No field. If enabled, this vehicle does not appear in the normal list and is spawned via the special menu. VIP Only: No Yes Yes/No field. If enabled, only VIPs can buy/see this vehicle (module-defined VIP logic). Allow Color Change: Yes No Yes/No field. Controls whether players can change the vehicle color in the dealer/customization UI. Factions (optional): Each row is one faction constant ID (e.g. FACTION_CITIZEN). Add Faction Allow Bodygroup Change: Yes No Yes/No field. Controls whether players can change vehicle bodygroups in the dealer/customization UI. Paint Job Price (optional): Optional override for the paint job/custom color price for this vehicle. Defaults to 300 if omitted. Repair Cost (optional): Optional override for how much it costs to repair this vehicle (only applies if your cardealer setup supports repairs). Generate Vehicle Item Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Vehicle Class: Entity class to spawn (must exist on server) Display Name: Name shown in the car spawner UI/menu. Description: A powerful car that can be used for transportation. Description shown to players (tooltip/details panel). Price: Purchase/spawn price in your schema currency. Spawn Distance: Optional distance offset used when spawning the vehicle (defaults to 200). Spawn Height: Optional height offset used when spawning the vehicle (defaults to 12). Category: Optional organization category shown in the spawner UI (defaults to "Vehicles"). Crate Model: Optional crate/placeholder model (defaults to a wood crate if omitted). Allowed Factions: Optional. Add faction unique IDs that can spawn this vehicle. Leave empty for all factions. Add Faction Generate Car Spawner Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Level: Clearance level number Pay Bonus: Extra pay/salary bonus granted for this clearance level (module-defined meaning).  Warned            If enabled, players of this clearance level receive hack alerts. Title: Short title/summary shown to players for this clearance level. Description: Enlisted access for standard operational areas. Longer description of what this clearance level represents or grants. Passes: How many passes/uses this clearance level provides (module-defined meaning). Speed Min: Minimum speed value for this clearance tier (used by module configuration). Speed Max: Maximum speed value for this clearance tier (used by module configuration). Width Green: Green width setting used by the module (UI/scan tuning). Width Blue: Blue width setting used by the module (UI/scan tuning). Generate Clearance Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Advanced Corpse Looting
docs.text: Comprehensive corpse management and looting system that creates realistic player corpses upon death with full inventory and money transfer. Features include: automatic corpse creation with player appearance preservation, dual inventory interface for seamless item transfer between player and corpse, bidirectional money deposit/withdrawal system with real-time synchronization, distance-based looting mechanics with anti-spam protection, multi-user corpse access with shared state management, automatic cleanup of corpse inventories on removal, and visual/audio feedback for all transactions. The system handles equipped items by unequipping them before transfer and supports both modern inventory instances and legacy item systems.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Advanced Crafting
docs.text: Comprehensive crafting system featuring multiple crafting stations (forge, workbench), recipe-based crafting with time-based progress bars, attribute requirements and skill progression, tool dependencies, faction restrictions, knowledge system with recipe books, dynamic UI with progress tracking and cancellation, item consumption and output with randomized quantities, and automatic entity registration for crafting stations

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Station Generator Recipe Generator Station ID: Used as the station key and to generate entity class lia_craftingstation_<id> Station Name: Display name shown to players for this crafting station. Model: World model used by the spawned crafting station entity. Generate Station Code Generate Example Station ID: Which station this recipe belongs to (must match a generated station ID). Recipe ID: Unique key for this recipe within the station. Recipe Name: Display name shown in the crafting menu. Craft Time: Time in seconds required to craft this recipe. Requires Knowledge: No Yes If enabled, this recipe requires knowledge to craft (module-defined meaning). Crafting Reward: How much crafting progression/reward to grant on completion (module-defined meaning). Requirements: Item uniqueID and amount. Add Requirement Output: Amount can be a number (1) or range ({2, 4}). Add Output Tools (optional): Each row is one required tool item uniqueID. Add Tool Attributes (optional): Each row becomes one entry in attributes ([attribKey] = minimum). Attribute: character attribute key (e.g. strength). Minimum: minimum value required. Add Attribute Faction (optional): Each row is one faction constant (e.g., FACTION_CITIZEN). Use multiple rows for lists. Add Faction Generate Recipe Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Handcuffs & Restraint
docs.text: Comprehensive player restraint system featuring handcuff items and weapons, rope-based tying mechanics, complete weapon and movement restrictions for restrained players, drag functionality with submodule support, blindfold and gag features, lockpicking mechanics, realistic restraint sounds, and robust hostage scenario tools for immersive roleplay interactions.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Daily Rewards
docs.text: Daily rewards system with 20 objectives (walking, chatting, playtime, money, interactions, jobs, building, vehicles, arrests, healing, property, lockpicking, paychecks, ads, emergencies, XP, voting, crimes, spending, sprinting) and escalating streak bonuses at 14/30/60/90 days.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Delivery Job
docs.text: A comprehensive delivery job system that provides players with parcel delivery missions. Features include a dedicated Delivery faction, randomly generated delivery locations across different maps, time-sensitive deliveries with visual indicators, delivery van spawning with custom bodygroups, crate pickup and drop-off mechanics, distance-based interaction prompts, monetary rewards for completed deliveries, visual waypoint markers showing delivery destinations, cooldown system between deliveries, and proper cleanup of delivery entities on disconnect or faction change. The system encourages map exploration and provides an engaging roleplaying experience for players seeking delivery work.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Data Storage
docs.text: Comprehensive data storage system featuring portable memory disks (64C-1024C capacity) with read/write functionality, password protection, and terminal/decoder integration. Includes physical disk entities, inventory items, and interactive terminals for data manipulation and storage.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Comprehensive Drug
docs.text: Complete drug economy and gameplay system featuring cultivation (weed planting with growth phases), processing (drug processor machines), consumption with various effects (speed boost, damage reduction, stamina regeneration, melee speed, needs decay reduction, ragdoll recovery), overdose mechanics with configurable chances, addiction system, visual effects HUD, and administrative controls. Includes 6 drug types: Cocaine, Heroin, Weed, Meth, LSD, and MDMA, each with unique properties and processing requirements.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Death Trooper Voice Scrambler
docs.text: Advanced voice communication scrambling system for the Death Trooper faction. Provides secure encrypted voice channels that prevent unauthorized players from hearing DT communications. When enabled, non-Death Trooper listeners cannot hear the voice chat and instead hear randomized static/scrambling sound effects. Authorized Death Trooper members and uplinked players can communicate normally while maintaining operational security. Features real-time voice filtering, multiple scrambling sound effects, and visual voice indicators showing scrambler status.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Dynamic Event
docs.text: Comprehensive dynamic event system that creates map-specific NPC encounters with automatic scheduling, configurable spawn positions with radius-based randomization, player count requirements, intelligent spawn validation using hull traces, staff commands for manual event control, and real-time player notifications. Supports multiple concurrent events per map with customizable NPCs, positions, and messages.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Extraction Flare
docs.text: Extraction flare system that allows players to signal extraction points by throwing throwable flares. Includes configurable extraction positions per map, flare grenade items, visual effects, and network messaging for extraction coordination. Provides raid-style extraction mechanics with countdown timers and escape zone functionality.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Faction PK
docs.text: Allows faction leaders to perform PKs on their members.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: No changelog available.

docs.title: Advanced Armors - Fantasy Edition
docs.text: Adds fantasy-themed armors with equippable protection stats, overlays, optional training requirements, and mod support. Features: damage resistance, movement/jump bonuses, fall protection, footstep sounds, armor mod installation/removal, and admin toggletraining command.

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Armor Generator Mod Generator Unique ID: Unique identifier for this armor (no spaces, lowercase) Armor Name: The name shown to players for this armor item. Description: Standard combat armor worn by Combine soldiers Shown in item tooltip/details. Icon Model: Shown in inventory (also defaults item model) Player Model: Applied to player when equipped (optional) Player Model Mode: Single Model Multi Model Multi Model uses an equivalency table compatible with lia.armors.resolvePlayerModel. Physical Resistance: 0-100 physical damage resistance Magic Resistance: 0-100 magical damage resistance Speed Boost: Negative slows, positive speeds up Jump Boost: Adjusts jump height while equipped (module-defined scaling). Takes Fall Damage If enabled, normal fall damage applies while this armor is equipped. Requires Training If enabled, players must meet the module's training requirement before equipping/using this armor. Overlay: Overlay to apply while this armor is equipped. Leave empty for none. Footstep Sound: Footstep sound used while this armor is equipped. Leave empty for nil (use default footstep sounds). Supported Mods: Each row is one mod type this armor supports (not uniqueIDs). Add Mod Model Equivalencies: Each row maps a current model to a replacement. Use key default or * Add Model Mapping Generate Armor Code Generate Example Unique ID: Unique identifier for this mod item Mod Type: This must match armor "supportedMods" entries Mod Name: The name shown to players for this mod item. Description: Increases physical damage resistance. Shown in item tooltip/details. Effect Description: A player-facing summary of what the mod does (does not affect stats by itself). Model: World/inventory model for the mod item. Physical Resist: Resistance value to apply via effects.physicalResist when the mod is installed (module-defined scaling). Magic Resist: Resistance value to apply via effects.magicResist when the mod is installed (module-defined scaling). Regen: Regeneration value to apply via effects.regen when the mod is installed (module-defined scaling). Speed: Speed modifier to apply via effects.speed when installed (negative slows, positive speeds up). Damage: Damage modifier to apply via effects.damage when installed (module-defined usage). Generate Mod Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Fence - Black Market Dealer
docs.text: A comprehensive fence NPC system that provides underground black market services. Features include: Purchase stolen items and contraband from players at fair market values, Dynamic pricing based on item reward values and rarity, Full integration with the robbery system for all stolen goods, Secure transaction processing with inventory validation, Interactive dialogue system with contextual responses, Buy and sell interface with item filtering, Support for weapons, valuables, and illegal items, Anti-theft protection and transaction logging.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Hunger & Thirst
docs.text: Comprehensive survival system that manages player hunger and thirst levels. Features dynamic food registration with customizable hunger/thirst values, stamina penalties for low hunger, automatic hunger degradation over time, and a variety of food items including canned goods, beverages, and MREs. Integrates with character factions and includes staff exemptions.

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Unique ID: Unique identifier for this item (no spaces, lowercase) Item Name: Name shown to players in the inventory UI. Description: A juicy burger cooked to perfection, served with fresh vegetables and a special sauce. Perfect for satisfying your hunger. Description shown to players (tooltip/details panel). Model: 3D model path for the food item. Hunger: How much hunger to restore when consumed (0-100). Thirst: How much thirst to restore when consumed (0-100). Width: Inventory grid width. Height: Inventory grid height. Category: Item category shown in inventory UI. Generate Food Item Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Comprehensive Resource Gathering & Crafting
docs.text: A complete resource gathering system featuring tree harvesting (spruce wood, sticks, tree sap, logs, wood planks) with axe-based chopping mechanics, ore mining from rocks (iron, gold, silver ore, coal, stone) for smelting into ingots, an interactive fishing system requiring poles and bait that yields various fish types (lake trout, bass, catfish, perch) plus junk items (old boots, trash), comprehensive crafting materials processing (raw resources into refined materials like iron ingots, wood planks, and weapons such as iron swords), and full stackable inventory management with configurable max quantities for all gathered items.

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Entity Class: Unique identifier for this gatherable entity (no spaces, lowercase) Display Name: Shown to players when looking at or interacting with the gatherable entity. Description: A rich vein of iron ore embedded in the rock face. Player-facing description for the gatherable entity. Model Path: World model used by the spawned gatherable entity. Hit Sound: Sound played when entity is hit (leave empty for default) Health: How many hits/damage units the entity can take before it is depleted. Items Before Cooldown: How many gather rewards can be obtained before the node goes on cooldown. Cooldown Time (seconds): How long the node stays inactive before it can be gathered again. Valid Weapons: Each row is one weapon class that can gather this entity. Add Weapon Loot Table: Each row becomes one entry in lootTable ([itemID] = { min = X, max = Y, chance = Z }). Item: the item unique ID to award (e.g. iron_ore). Min/Max: random quantity range to give when this item drops (inclusive). Chance: percent chance (0-100) to drop this item per gather reward roll. Add Loot Item Generate Gathering Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: City Guide NPC
docs.text: Implements a comprehensive City Guide NPC system that provides new player orientation and navigation assistance. Features include interactive dialog trees with multiple conversation paths, automatic waypoint marking for important city landmarks (Downtown, Central Park), detailed location information, and contextual guidance about the city

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Advanced Identification & Character Registry
docs.text: Comprehensive identification system featuring customizable character profiles with physical attributes (age, sex, ethnicity, weight, eye/hair color, blood type), multiple regional ID card designs (California, German, Florida, New York, Southside, Yorkshire), character recognition mechanics, and interactive ID viewing/requesting functionality. Includes gender-based model filtering, persistent character data storage, and administrative ID management tools for immersive roleplaying scenarios.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Comprehensive Injury
docs.text: A comprehensive injury management system featuring multiple injury types including broken legs, bleeding wounds, head concussions, and pain effects. Each injury has unique side effects, healing requirements, and medical treatment options. Includes medical items, treatment minigames, and persistent injury tracking across player sessions.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Character Progression
docs.text: A comprehensive character progression system featuring experience points, level advancement, and a skill tree system. Players earn XP through various activities, level up to gain skill points, and invest in three distinct skill paths: Endurance (increases maximum health by 25), Combat Training (boosts weapon damage by 15%), and Engineering (enhances tool effectiveness by 15% and repair speed by 30%). The system includes skill dependencies requiring certain levels, full F1 menu integration for skill management, network synchronization for multiplayer functionality, and administrative tools for XP/skill adjustments. Engineering skill requires Endurance tier 1 as a prerequisite, creating meaningful progression choices.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Limb Damage
docs.text: Advanced limb damage system that simulates realistic leg injuries. When players are shot in the legs (left or right leg hitgroups), they experience a temporary sprint disability with configurable duration. Features include automatic healing after the damage duration, visual HUD indicators with red screen effects and text warnings, server-side damage validation with blast damage immunity, toggleable system enable/disable functionality, and proper cleanup on player disconnection. The system uses networked variables for synchronized client-server state and provides immersive gameplay feedback through notifications and visual effects.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Interactive Looting
docs.text: A comprehensive looting system that creates searchable container entities throughout the world. Features dynamic entity registration, skill-based access requirements (strength/luck attributes), multi-tiered rarity system (Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary/Unique) with weighted probability distribution, intelligent reward allocation with inventory management, customizable container types (cardboard boxes, crates, barrels, lockers, safes), cooldown timers for respawn prevention, audio feedback systems, and seamless integration with the Lilia framework

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Unique ID: Unique identifier passed to lia.loot.registerLoot. Name: Shown to players for this loot container type. Model: World model for the loot container entity. Opening Sound: Played when the container is opened/searched. Chance: Base chance value used by the looting system (module-defined meaning; commonly treated as a percent or weight). Chance Time: Additional timing/chance configuration used by the module (schema/module-defined; keep consistent with other loot definitions). Attributes Requirements: Each row becomes one entry in skillRequirements ([attribKey] = requiredValue). Attribute: character attribute key (e.g. strength, luck). Required: minimum value required to search this container. Add Attribute Rarity Tiers: Add tiers like Common/Uncommon/Rare/etc with an overall chance and item list. Add Tier Generate Loot Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: LSCS Lightsaber
docs.text: Integrates LSCS (Lightsaber Combat System) framework with Lilia, providing comprehensive lightsaber mechanics including customizable hilts, crystals, stances, and abilities. Features dual-wielding support, inventory management, and seamless character data synchronization for enhanced Jedi/Sith roleplay combat experience.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Player Marketplace
docs.text: A comprehensive player-to-player trading marketplace system featuring persistent database storage, configurable listing limits and pricing controls, intuitive GUI interfaces for buyers and sellers, administrative management tools with item removal and price modification capabilities, automatic economic transactions with money transfers, NPC vendor integration for marketplace access, real-time listing updates and search functionality, item validation and blacklist support, and robust server economy integration.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Comprehensive Medal
docs.text: A comprehensive medal award and display system featuring multiple themed medal packs (1942 RP, US Military branches, Police departments, Star Wars RP), persistent character-based medal storage with rarity tiers (Common, Uncommon, Rare, Exceptionally Rare), staff permissions for medal management, wearable medal slots (up to 5 medals), character profile integration, and admin controls for giving/taking medals with full network synchronization

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Medal ID: Unique identifier/key for this medal. Medal Name: Display name shown to players. Description: Awarded for attaining Patrol Level 1 in the LAPD. Description shown to players (tooltip/details panel). Icon filename: Filename inside medals pack folder. Use lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores only. Size W: Icon render width in pixels. Size H: Icon render height in pixels. Rarity Type: Common Uncommon Rare Legendary Controls rarity styling/labeling for this medal. Generate Medal Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Medical & Wound
docs.text: Comprehensive medical system featuring persistent wound tracking that reduces max health, a configurable medkit SWEP for healing self/others with cooldowns, and a surgery system requiring certified providers and surgical kits to treat lingering wounds. Players accumulate wounds on death that permanently decrease maximum health until treated through surgery.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Advanced Police Suite
docs.text: Complete law enforcement management system featuring 23-tier police rank hierarchy with progressive salaries and equipment kits, comprehensive criminal code with detailed offenses and sentencing guidelines, advanced police computer database with real-time criminal records, warrant management, and serial number tracking, specialized police equipment including tasers, nightsticks, and finebooks, dynamic jail cell management system with map-specific configurations, police locker and NPC integration, dispatcher system for citizen help requests, internal affairs oversight, and extensive administrative privileges for police faction management.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Prop-Based Building
docs.text: Advanced prop-based construction system enabling players to craft and place diverse furniture items including chairs, tables, couches, storage units, and decorative props. Features resource requirements, health systems, destruction mechanics with specialized tools, and supports upgrades into functional entities for comprehensive base building and interior customization.

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Prop Generator Item Generator Unique ID: Unique identifier for this prop (no spaces, lowercase) Prop Name: Name shown to players for this constructable prop entry. Description: A simple wooden chair for sitting. Shown in UI/tooltip for this constructable prop. Prop Model: Model placed in the world when constructed Inventory Model (Optional): Model shown in inventory (leave empty for default: item_item_crate.mdl) Inventory Width: Inventory grid width for the item representation (defaults to 2). Inventory Height: Inventory grid height for the item representation (defaults to 2). Health: Optional health value for the placed prop (0 = indestructible). Category: Item category (defaults to "Constructable") Generate Prop Code Generate Example Unique ID: Unique identifier for this item Item Name: Name shown to players for the generated item. Description: A custom construction item. Shown in item tooltip/details. Item Model: Model shown in inventory Prop Model: Model placed when constructed Inventory Width: Inventory grid width for the item. Inventory Height: Inventory grid height for the item. Prop Health: Health of the placed prop (optional) Generate Item Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Advanced Radio Communication
docs.text: Comprehensive radio communication framework featuring handheld radios and stationary broadcast units with frequency tuning, encrypted faction channels, preset stations, range-based transmission, static noise effects, and Star Wars RP comlink compatibility. Supports both item-based and entity-based radio devices with real-time voice transmission, channel management, and secure communications for different factions.

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Preset Generator Encrypted Generator Frequency: Radio frequency for this preset. Preset Name: Label shown for this preset in the radio UI (frequency remains the actual channel identifier). Generate Preset Code Generate Example Frequency: Radio frequency for this encrypted channel. Channel Name: Optional name shown in the radio UI for the encrypted channel; if empty, the frequency is typically shown by itself (module-defined behavior). Factions: Each row is one faction constant that can access this frequency. Add Faction Generate Encrypted Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo

docs.title: Class Ranking
docs.text: Comprehensive hierarchical ranking system that manages player positions within classes. Provides promotion, demotion, hiring, and kicking functionality with tier-based permissions. Automatically assigns rank-specific weapons, clearance levels, and models. Includes administrative commands and privilege-based access control for class management.

docs.title: Generator
docs.text: Class Constant: The top-level key inside lia.ranking.rankTable for the class you are defining ranks for. Rank ID: Unique key for this rank within the selected class; used to reference/promote/demote to this rank. Rank Tag: Rank abbreviation/shorthand shown in UI/overhead displays (module-defined usage). Rank Name: The full readable name shown for this rank. Model: Player model applied/used for this rank (module-defined behavior). Clearance: Clearance level required/granted for this rank (used by clearance/door systems if your schema ties them together). Salary: Salary/pay value associated with this rank (how/when it is paid is schema/module-defined). Tier: Tier/order value for this rank (commonly used to compare seniority; higher usually means higher rank). Weapons: Each row is one swep classname entry in the "Weapons" list. Add Weapon Can Promote If enabled, players of this rank can promote other members. Can Demote If enabled, players of this rank can demote other members. Can Hire If enabled, players of this rank can hire/recruit into the class. Can Kick If enabled, players of this rank can remove members from the class. Generate Rank Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Realtor - Property Management
docs.text: Comprehensive property creation and management system featuring a specialized Property Creator weapon for intuitive door selection. Supports temporary rentals with daily pricing and permanent faction-owned properties with database persistence, preview positioning, and multi-door property compilation.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Job Recruiter NPC
docs.text: Automated job recruitment system that dynamically creates recruiter NPCs for all configured factions. Each NPC provides contextual dialog options allowing players to join jobs (with proper faction transfer hooks and loadout updates) or resign (reverting to default faction). Features intelligent option visibility based on player

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Referrals
docs.text: Player referral system allowing players to refer others and earn monetary rewards when referred players join, with configurable reward amounts and admin management tools.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: FOB Respawn
docs.text: A comprehensive Forward Operating Base (FOB) respawn management system that allows administrators to place faction-specific respawn terminals. Players can respawn at designated FOB points based on their faction affiliation, with support for multiple spawn points per faction and map-specific configurations. The system includes client-server synchronization, administrative placement tools, and automatic respawn location selection.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Store Robbery
docs.text: Comprehensive store robbery system featuring interactive jewelry cases and cash registers with timed robbery mechanics. Players can steal various valuable items including cash bundles (0-500 value), jewelry bags (100-300 value), gold watches (50-150 value), and diamond rings (200-500 value) with weighted probability drops. The system includes police requirements (minimum 1-2 officers), configurable respawn timers (15-30 minutes), cooldown states, inventory management, and faction blacklist support. Features visual feedback with entity information display, action progress bars, and success/failure notifications.

docs.title: Generator
docs.text:
Robbery Location Generator

Create new robbery locations with configurable settings and faction restrictions.

Entity Class: Used as the entity class name for lia.robberies.registerEntity() and becomes the spawned entity class. Name: Player-facing name shown for this robbery location/entity. Model: World model used by the robbery entity. Min Police: Minimum number of police players required online to start the robbery. Robbing Timer: Seconds required to complete the robbery interaction. Respawn Timer: Cooldown time before this robbery can be performed again. Blacklisted Factions: Each row is one faction constant (e.g., FACTION_CITIZEN). Add Faction Stealable Items: Each row is one entry in the "stealable" list (chance is 1-100). Add Stealable Item Generate Location Code Generate Example Generated Code

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Taxi Transportation Service
docs.text: A comprehensive taxi transportation system featuring an NPC dispatcher that spawns taxi vehicles for player hire, provides instant teleportation to predefined city locations with distance-based pricing, prevents duplicate taxi ownership, and integrates custom workshop vehicle models for realistic taxi services.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Territories - Faction Control Point
docs.text: Comprehensive territory control system that allows factions to capture, defend, and hold strategic control points. Features include configurable capture zones with customizable radius and capture timers, automatic NPC defender spawning, real-time HUD status displays showing current ownership and capture progress, faction-based ownership mechanics with collision prevention to ensure proper point distribution, reward systems for holding territories, persistent entity saving, and administrative tools for managing control points. The system supports dynamic territory warfare where factions can contest control points through presence-based capture mechanics requiring minimum defenders to secure areas.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Terrorism
docs.text: Comprehensive terrorism system featuring vehicle car bombs that detonate on engine start and door breach explosives. Both bomb types support timed detonation (0-60 seconds) and remote detonation using handheld detonators. Includes realistic explosion physics with debris creation, player interaction menus for remote detonation, and a complete item system with explosive devices and detonators. Perfect for sabotage operations, controlled demolitions, and asymmetric warfare scenarios.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Version 1.0
  • Initial Release

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.
